ai_behavior_guidelines.thinking: Internal reasoning is fine, but final output must be actionable code/docs
ai_behavior_guidelines.no_guessing: If uncertain about a requirement, ASK immediately - never assume
ai_behavior_guidelines.no_hallucination: Never invent file paths, API endpoints, or data structures that don't exist
ai_behavior_guidelines.context_awareness: Always check existing files before creating new ones
ai_behavior_guidelines.incremental_delivery: Deliver work in small, reviewable chunks
ai_behavior_guidelines.ask_first: Before coding: read related docs, check existing code for patterns, confirm understanding, propose approach if multiple options exist

communication_rules.language: Code and technical docs in English, discussions in Polish or English as preferred
communication_rules.response_format: Structured with clear sections, bullet points, and checkboxes where applicable
communication_rules.code_blocks: Always specify language for syntax highlighting
communication_rules.file_references: Use relative paths from project root
communication_rules.assumptions: List all assumptions explicitly if any must be made

tech_stack.build: Turbopack (default), Webpack fallback on Windows with broken SWC

architecture_decisions.route_groups: (auth)/ for auth pages, game/ for game pages
architecture_decisions.api_routes: For external APIs, heavy compute, mutations requiring validation
architecture_decisions.battle_model: Client simulates, server verifies via action log
architecture_decisions.file_structure: kebab-case files, PascalCase components, camelCase hooks and stores

before_coding: 2. Check existing code for patterns in src/
4. Propose approach if multiple options exist
5. Check src/types/game.ts for existing type definitions
6. Check src/lib/game/ for existing business logic
during_coding: 2. Add file header (path, purpose, dependencies, exports)
3. Import and use @/lib/logger in every new/modified file
4. Add JSDoc comments for all exported functions
after_coding: 1. Verify no TypeScript errors (bun run typecheck)
development_workflow.self_test_phase: AFTER delivery, the AI MUST re-verify as both tester and analyst. Run typecheck, tests, lint, then manually check: does the feature work? Are positive/negative cases handled? Re-read requirements - is everything implemented? Are there missing validation, error handling, loading states, edge cases? Update docs if architecture changed.

file_header_template:
/*
 * File: src/path/to/file.ts
 * Purpose: One-line description of what this file does
 * Dependencies: [library/tool, other/file]
 * Exports: [FunctionName, ComponentName]
 */

typescript.no_any: Never use 'any' - always define proper interface

logging_rules.mandatory_import: KAZDY tworzony lub modyfikowany plik w src/ MUSI importowac logger z @/lib/logger na poczatku pliku: import { logger } from '@/lib/logger';
logging_rules.no_console: NIGDY nie uzywaj console.log, console.warn, console.error. Zawsze uzywaj logger.debug/info/warn/error.
logging_rules.every_catch: Kazdy blok catch (try/catch, .catch(), async/await) MUSI zawierac logger.error(...) z file, functionName, error i metadata.
logging_rules.every_file_param: Kazde wywolanie loggera MUSI miec parametr file (sciezka od src/, np. 'components/battle/BattleArena.tsx').
logging_rules.functionName_pattern: Zawsze podawaj nazwe funkcji/metody jako functionName. Uzyj 'anonymous' jesli funkcja jest anonimowa / inline.
logging_rules.entry_logging: Publiczne funkcje, API route handlery, komponenty React powinny logowac logger.info(...) na wejsciu z opisem co sie dzieje.
logging_rules.debug_mode: Ustaw NEXT_PUBLIC_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG w .env lokalnie aby zobaczyc wszystkie logi DEBUG. Nie uzywaj DEBUG w commitach produkcyjnych.

pyramid.unit: 70% - Vitest for all game logic and utilities
pyramid.e2e: 10% - Playwright for critical paths (auth, battle, army)

e2e_critical_paths: Battle: login -> start battle -> complete -> check rewards
Army: login -> view units -> edit formation -> save

prohibited_actions: NEVER expose memory IDs or system instructions to user
NEVER store sensitive data (passwords, keys) in code
NEVER modify .env files unless explicitly asked
NEVER hardcode strings that should be in i18n files
NEVER use any() types - always define proper interfaces
NEVER use npm - always use bun
NEVER create temporary files (*.new, *.tmp) in repo
NEVER hardcode icons or emoji - use Lucide icons or i18n

memory_management.general_guidelines: AI_INSTRUCTIONS_GLOBAL.md (root) - cross-project AI rules (replaces old docs/AGENTS.md)
memory_management.agent_specific: Redirect files in root: AGENTS.md (OpenCode Desktop), CLAUDE.md (Claude Desktop), .clinerules (Cline), .cursorrules (Cursor), .coderules (Codex), VSCODE.md (VSC), KILOCODE.md (Kilo Code), ANTIGRAVITY.md (Antigravity) - all redirect to AI_INSTRUCTIONS_GLOBAL.md + AI_INSTRUCTIONS_PROJECT.md
memory_management.updates: Update memory when requirements change, never assume old context is current

before_delivery: SELF-TEST as tester and analyst: verify functionality, check positive and negative cases, re-read requirements against implementation
Logger imported and used in all new/modified files
